books.title: The Black Star Passes
books.author: John W. Campbell
books.summary: Three scientists from Earth invent space travel and meet extraterrestrial life, but then must defend the solar system from a new threat.

books.title: The Narrative of Arthur Gordon Pym of Nantucket
books.author: Edgar Allan Poe
books.summary: A young stowaway embarks on high-seas adventures.

books.title: Passages from the Life of a Philosopher
books.author: Charles Babbage
books.summary: The eminent Victorian scientist and mathematician on his inventions, dreams and adventures.

books.title: Eminent Victorians
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: Four witty and biting biographies of Victorians that take aim at the moral superiority of their subject’s time.

books.title: The Railway Children
books.author: E. Nesbit
books.summary: After their father is forced to leave them, three children and their mother must live in near-poverty in the country, where their lives are enlivened by the nearby presence of a railway line.

books.title: The Conscious Lovers
books.author: Richard Steele
books.summary: A rich young man tries to avoid the marriage his father has arranged.

books.title: Candida
books.author: George Bernard Shaw
books.summary: A clergyman’s beliefs about his respectable household are challenged by a young poet who is in love with his wife.

books.title: The Gods of Mars
books.author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
books.summary: John Carter returns to Mars to try and determine the fate of his lost love Dejah Thoris.

books.title: The Secret of the Caves
books.author: Franklin W. Dixon
books.summary: Four boys vacation at nearby caves only to be swept up in a mystery.

books.title: The Wolf-Leader
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.summary: A shoemaker makes a compact with a black werewolf to exchange strands of his hair for granted wishes.

books.title: Roughing It
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: Mark Twain’s semiautobiographical account of his travel across the United States.

books.title: The Brooklyn Murders
books.author: G. D. H. Cole
books.summary: Scotland Yard and a pair of amateur sleuths investigate the case of two heirs to a fortune who apparently murdered each other.

books.title: Polynesian Mythology
books.author: George Grey
books.summary: A collection of Māori mythology covering the start of the universe through to the spread of Māori tribes through New Zealand’s North Island.

books.title: The Everlasting Man
books.author: G. K. Chesterton
books.summary: G. K. Chesterton takes us through the history of man, rebuking common secular views and presenting the case for the Catholic Church.

books.title: Poetry
books.author: Matthew Arnold
books.summary: A collection of occasional and dramatic poems by a Victorian poet, educator, and critic.

books.title: The Indiscreet Jewels
books.author: Denis Diderot
books.summary: The Sultan of the Congo brings about philosophical inquiry and scandal when he is granted a magical ring that permits him to compel speech from the vaginas of the women of his court.

books.title: Pellucidar
books.author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
books.summary: An adventurer returns to the hollow interior of the Earth in search of his lost love.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Frank Belknap Long
books.summary: A collection of short fiction by Frank Belknap Long, ordered by date of original publication.

books.title: The Pilgrim’s Progress
books.author: John Bunyan
books.summary: An allegorical story of a pilgrim in search of redemption, followed later by his wife and children, passing through great trials before reaching the Celestial City.

books.title: Two Years Before the Mast
books.author: Richard Henry Dana Jr.
books.summary: A Harvard student embarks on a two-year voyage as a sailor aboard a merchant ship, exposing the harsh conditions of life at sea in the 19th century.
